title: Bogoliubov sum rules and the Knight-shift ellipsoid in noncentrosymmetric superconductors
abstract: We show that the residual $T=0$ Knight shift of a noncentrosymmetric superconductor in the strong-locking regime is completely determined by a single Fermi-surface average -- the projector $Π_{μν}=\langle\hat n_μ\hat n_ν\rangle_{\rm FS}$ of the spin-locking direction $\hat n_{\mathbf{k}}$ -- giving the tensor identity $χ_{μν}(0)=χ_{N}[δ_{μν}-Π_{μν}]$ independently of pairing symmetry, gap magnitude, and Fermi-surface shape. Because $\mathrm{Tr}\,Π=1$, the three principal Knight shifts at $T=0$ lie on a two-dimensional simplex of locking textures, the \emph{Knight-shift ellipsoid}, whose vertices, edges, and interior classify every canonical pairing class. The identity follows from a Bogoliubov sum rule, $\sum|M_{ph,O}|^{2}+\sum|M_{pp,O}|^{2}=\mathrm{Tr}_{s}(O^{2})$, valid at every momentum for every Hermitian single-particle operator $O$ as the BdG-doubled form of unitary invariance. Around the central theorem we develop controlled departures (a closed-form $s$-wave SOC interpolation, a finite-field strong-locking identity), a dynamical counterpart (a spin Ferrell--Glover--Tinkham sum rule and a rigorous vanishing-projection theorem for $1/T_{1}$), and a decoupled-pocket multiband baseline, packaged into six experimental protocols. Applied to the $^{75}$As NMR data on K$_{2}$Cr$_{3}$As$_{3}$, the observed ellipsoid sits at the oblate-axial vertex $(0,0,1)$ and saturates the trace bound; the decoupled-pocket SOC-texture baseline is excluded by $\sim 0.5$ in normalized units, requiring a common $\hat c$-axis locking on all three pockets, and the suppression of $1/T_{1}\parallel\hat c$ is identified, via the vanishing-projection theorem, as a fingerprint of finite-$\mathbf{q}$ ferromagnetic spin-fluctuation gap formation.
summary: The paper introduces a geometric framework to classify pairing symmetries in noncentrosymmetric superconductors by relating the zero-temperature Knight shift to the spin-locking texture of the Fermi surface. It provides a set of experimental protocols to distinguish between various singlet and triplet pairing states. The theory is validated by analyzing NMR data on K2Cr3As3, revealing a specific c-axis spin-locking mechanism.
main_problem: Determining the pairing symmetry and spin-orbit coupling (SOC) texture in noncentrosymmetric superconductors using the residual T=0 Knight shift.
main_result: Derivation of a central theorem where the T=0 Knight shift tensor is determined solely by the Fermi-surface average of the spin-locking direction, independent of pairing symmetry or gap magnitude, and application to K2Cr3As3 to identify a c-axis locking texture.
method: Use of a Bogoliubov sum rule within a BdG framework to establish geometric identities and the development of six experimental protocols for diagnosing pairing classes via a Knight-shift ellipsoid.
model_or_system: Noncentrosymmetric superconductors, specifically the K2Cr3As3 system, modeled using a single-band unitary Bogoliubov-de Gennes (BdG) framework with antisymmetric spin-orbit coupling.
key_observables: Residual T=0 Knight shift tensor, spin susceptibility, spin-lattice relaxation rate (1/T1), and the Knight-shift ellipsoid.
important_parameters: Spin-locking direction (n_k), SOC strength (lambda), Zeeman field (h), and the projection tensor (Pi_munu).
main_assumptions: The primary derivation assumes the strong-locking regime and unitary pairing; the application assumes a single-band model and hyperfine-invariant coupling.
figures_summary: Figure 4 shows a numerical cross-check of the derived closed-form theory against digitized endpoints of s-wave curves, demonstrating high precision.
paper_structure: The paper establishes a central Bogoliubov sum rule, derives static bounds and the Knight-shift ellipsoid geometry, explores departures in intermediate SOC and finite Zeeman fields, develops dynamical counterparts (FGT sum rule), and concludes with experimental protocols applied to K2Cr3As3 data.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
